column2: The process of identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks to the integrity and availability of products and services.
A nested inventory of software components, libraries, and dependencies.
Contractual commitment defining service performance metrics (e.g., 99.9% uptime) and penalties.
A foundational contract that governs future transactions and work orders between parties.
Risks originating from suppliers or providers earlier in the supply chain (e.g., component manufacturers).
Risks associated with the distribution and delivery of a product/service to the end customer.
Focuses on controls relevant to a user entity's internal control over financial reporting (ICFR).
Focuses on Trust Services Criteria: Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy.
A contract provision that allows a customer to verify a vendor's compliance through inspections or audits.
Unauthorized or fraudulent hardware components that can introduce vulnerabilities or failures.
